evidence.coopMechanism: Same delta-submission path as crit_lens issue

reproductionSteps: Two co-op peers, at least one holding obsidian_dice relic
One client rolls the +50% branch, other rolls the -25% branch
Deltas diverge — merged result is the arithmetic sum of the two divergent outcomes
suggestedFix: Same pattern as crit_lens: use seeded RNG fork for randomness that affects shared enemy state. Alternatively, include the relic roll result in the delta payload so both sides converge.

title: relicAcquisitionService uses Math.random() — co-op reward screens could offer different relics
description: relicAcquisitionService.ts lines 73 and 93 use Math.random() for rarity roll and candidate selection when offering post-combat relic rewards. If co-op has or will have a shared reward screen (the architecture doc is ambiguous on this), both clients will independently roll the relic pool and offer different relics. One player sees Rare Relic A, the other sees Common Relic B.

evidence.code: const roll = Math.random() * totalWeight; ... const pick = candidates[Math.floor(Math.random() * candidates.length)];

reproductionSteps: Host and guest see different relics offered
suggestedFix: Use getRunRng('relicRewards').next() instead of Math.random(), seeded from the run seed. The host can also broadcast the relic offer list to the guest at reward screen entry.

title: Attempt A (2-peer Docker lockstep) not feasible — BroadcastChannel transport cannot cross container boundaries
description: The multiplayer transport implementation (multiplayerTransport.ts) uses BroadcastChannel for peer-to-peer messaging. BroadcastChannel is a browser same-origin API — messages only reach tabs within the same browser context. Two separate Docker containers each have isolated Chromium instances. No cross-container BroadcastChannel is possible without a shared WebSocket server or similar bridge. Therefore, a true 2-peer headless lockstep test cannot be conducted with the current infrastructure. The test was downgraded to Attempt B (single-peer seed determinism via Vitest).

evidence.mechanism: BroadcastChannel API — tab-scoped, not cross-process or cross-container

reproductionSteps: Attempt to have both join the same BroadcastChannel
Messages will not be received across containers
suggestedFix: For headless integration testing of multiplayer, replace BroadcastChannel with a WebSocket transport mode (already partially scaffolded) and provide a test harness that injects a shared in-process message bus for two simulated peers.

title: DungeonMap nodes render below viewport — map appears near-black on first load
description: When dungeonMap loads via scenario, all map nodes are positioned below y=1080 (starting at y=1233). The visible viewport (1080px tall) shows only the fog-overlay wisps against a dark background. The map content is scrolled off-screen downward. A player arriving at the dungeon map for the first time would see a nearly black screen with fog wisps and no map nodes. The layout dump confirms nodes exist at y=693–1233, all below viewport. This could be an init timing issue (scroll position not set) or a CSS scroll-to-current-floor behavior that isn't working on first load.
reproductionSteps: Observe: screenshot shows near-black with fog wisps, no map nodes visible
Layout dump: all map-node buttons at y=693–1233, viewport height=1080
suggestedFix: Check DungeonMap.svelte scroll initialization — ensure the scroll container scrolls to the current floor row on mount. The nodes exist but are off-screen. May need a scrollToCurrentFloor() call after map renders.

title: relicSwapOverlay scenario loads specialEvent instead of RelicSwapOverlay component
description: The 'relic-swap' scenario (screen: relicSwapOverlay, 4 relics equipped) does not show the RelicSwapOverlay component. Instead, the layout dump shows div.special-overlay (specialEvent 'Deck Thin' event). The relicSwapOverlay may require a pendingSwapRelicId to render, and without it falls through to a different screen or the previous screen state bleeds through. The screenshot confirms 'Deck Thin' specialEvent is what renders, not any relic swap UI.
reproductionSteps: Observe: specialEvent 'Deck Thin' overlay renders instead of RelicSwapOverlay
suggestedFix: The scenarioSimulator's relic-swap preset should also set a pendingSwapRelicId in the run state, or the scenario should trigger the swap flow. Without a pending relic offer, RelicSwapOverlay has nothing to show.

title: multiplayerLobby renders empty — requires currentLobby !== null to mount
description: When navigating to multiplayerLobby via loadCustom({ screen: 'multiplayerLobby' }), the layout dump shows only a screen-transition div with no MultiplayerLobby component. Per docs, MultiplayerLobby only mounts when currentLobby !== null. The scenario simulator has no way to set up a lobby object, so the screen cannot be tested via scenario. The player sees a near-black screen with star particle background.
reproductionSteps: Observe: empty screen, layout dump shows only screen-transition element
suggestedFix: Add a multiplayerLobby scenario preset that bootstraps a mock lobby object (currentLobby) before navigating to the screen. The MultiplayerLobby component has a guard condition requiring currentLobby to be non-null.

title: masteryChallenge renders with very small viewport — dialog box uses ~50% of 1080p space
description: The mastery challenge dialog box occupies roughly the center 30% of the screen (approximately 600x420px out of 1920x1080). The surrounding 70% of the screen is pure black background. For a Steam PC target of 1920x1080, this creates a lot of dead space. The quiz panel appears correctly formed but undersized relative to the available viewport. The question text 'What is the approximate speed of light in a vacuum?' and 4 answer options are readable but small.
reproductionSteps: Observe: small quiz panel centered in large black void
suggestedFix: Consider expanding the masteryChallenge overlay to use more of the 1920x1080 space, or adding a background visual to fill the dark void.

title: restStudy empty state has no background ambience — pure dark void behind dialog
description: The restStudy empty state ('No Cards to Review') shows correct empty state messaging and a 'Return to Hub' escape button (per HIGH-8 contract), but the background is pure dark navy with no Phaser scene or background visual. Other screens in the same context (restRoom, restMeditate) show the dungeon/rest-site background. The empty state appears visually disconnected from the game world.
reproductionSteps: Observe: 'No Cards to Review' dialog on pure dark background
suggestedFix: Consider adding a background visual or keeping the rest-site Phaser scene active behind the empty state.

title: cardReward cards have empty description area — card text boxes appear blank
description: The cardReward screen shows 3 card choices with artwork visible, but the lower portion of each card (description/effect text area) appears as a dark empty rectangle. Card names and type icons are visible but the mechanical description text area shows no text. This could be due to test scenario facts not being populated, or the card description rendering not triggering in the test environment.
reproductionSteps: Observe: card artwork visible but description text area appears empty
suggestedFix: Check if card description text rendering requires the quiz question to be resolved first, or if there's a missing data hydration step.

title: runEnd screen uses narrow centered column — large empty margins on 1920x1080
description: The runEnd victory screen shows content in a narrow centered column (approximately 800px wide) centered on the 1920px canvas. Large empty margins on both sides (~560px each). Sections visible: 'FOES VANQUISHED' (enemy icons row), 'KNOWLEDGE HARVEST' (progress bars), 'RUN STATS' (stat tiles), and 'EASY MATTER 500' gold reward. Content is functional but the layout uses only ~42% of the available horizontal space.
reproductionSteps: Observe: narrow column centered in wide viewport with large empty margins
suggestedFix: Consider expanding runEnd layout to utilize more horizontal space on 1920px wide screens, or adding side panels (e.g., detailed fact review, achievements unlocked).

title: runPreview shows 'Preparing your knowledge chains...' loading state — chains never render
description: The runPreview (Your Knowledge Chains) screen shows a perpetual loading state: 'Preparing your knowledge chains...' text in the center. The 'Shuffle Chains' and 'Begin Expedition' buttons are visible at the bottom, and the FSRS legend (N/L/R/M) is at bottom-left. However, no chain distribution is shown — the main content area is empty. This indicates the chainDistribution data is not available when loaded via loadCustom.
reproductionSteps: Observe: 'Preparing your knowledge chains...' with empty chain distribution area
suggestedFix: The runPreview scenario needs a chainDistribution object in activeRunState. Add a dedicated runPreview scenario preset that bootstraps proper chain distribution data before navigating.

title: proceduralStudy shows error state 'Could not start practice session' — no procedural deck registered
description: The proceduralStudy screen shows an error: 'Could not start practice session. You'll need to register a procedural deck.' with a 'Go Back' button. This is an expected error state in the scenario context (no procedural deck is registered), but the error message is unclear to a regular player about how to register a procedural deck. The error handling provides an escape (Go Back) which is correct.
reproductionSteps: Observe: error state 'Could not start practice session'
suggestedFix: Improve error message to be more user-friendly (e.g., 'This mode requires a math practice deck. Visit the Study Temple to set one up.'). Also add a procedural study scenario preset that bootstraps the required deck.

title: profile screen shows sparse content on fresh save — sections appear to float in empty space
description: The Scholar's Profile screen shows a minimal layout with a fresh/empty save: Explorer avatar, Level 1, stats all at zero, and an 'No expeditions yet' placeholder. The layout uses 2-column grid but with no expedition data, many sections are minimal or absent. The visual result is a sparsely populated screen that could confuse new players about what content will appear here. The overall visual polish is functional but the empty state could be more welcoming.
reproductionSteps: Observe: sparse layout with zero stats, minimal section content
suggestedFix: Add richer empty-state messaging for profile sections (e.g., 'Complete your first expedition to see stats here'). Consider a 'teaser' layout that shows what each section will contain.

title: Hub sprite-hitbox buttons have no accessible name — all 8 unlabeled
description: 8 `button.sprite-hitbox` elements on the hub screen have no text content, no aria-label, and no title attribute. They are the primary navigation for Library, Camp, Tent, Shop, etc. Screen reader users and keyboard-only users cannot determine what each button does. The only labeled button is btn-start-run.

evidence.required: Every button must have accessible name via text, aria-label, or aria-labelledby

reproductionSteps: Inspect all button.sprite-hitbox elements in DevTools
Observe: no aria-label, no text content, no title attribute
suggestedFix: Add aria-label attributes matching the visual room/object names (e.g. aria-label='Library', aria-label='Rest Camp')

title: Hub HUD counters (fire streak, grey matter) are 14px with icon only — no label
description: The two HUD pills in the hub show numeric values (0 and 0) with emoji icons (fire, gem) but no text labels. The values are 14px which is just at the minimum threshold, and a new player has no context for what these numbers represent. The fire counter and grey matter resource are core game economy but not labeled.

evidence.required: Primary resource indicators should have labels on first encounter, or a persistent tooltip trigger

reproductionSteps: Observe top-center HUD pills showing fire emoji and gem emoji with number values
Note: no tooltip, no label, no explanation of what these values mean
suggestedFix: Add aria-label to hud pills (e.g. 'Streak: 0', 'Grey Matter: 0'), and add a first-visit tooltip

title: Start Run button at Y=119 is in top 11% of screen — far above thumb zone
description: btn-start-run is positioned at Y=119 (of 1080 total), placing it at 11% from the top. The thumb zone threshold (55% of screen height) is at Y=594. Primary actions should be in the bottom half for landscape card game ergonomics. The door/dungeon entrance is the most important button on the hub and is in the most difficult reach zone for handheld/Steam Deck players.

evidence.required: Primary actions should be in bottom 55% of screen (Y > 594px)

reproductionSteps: Observe btn-start-run position at top of screen
suggestedFix: This is a design constraint (the door is in the scene art). Consider adding a floating 'Start Run' CTA button lower on screen that supplements the sprite hitbox.

title: btn-end-turn has no visible accessible name — text is CSS-only 'END TURN' but element is HIDDEN per layout dump
description: The btn-end-turn element is flagged HIDDEN in the layout dump at position (24, 987) 197x69. This could be a z-index or CSS visibility issue causing the button to be absent from the accessibility tree. However, visually it renders correctly in the screenshot. If the HIDDEN flag indicates pointer-events:none or visibility:hidden, keyboard users cannot reach it.

evidence.measured: Flagged HIDDEN in layout dump, position (24, 987) 197x69
evidence.required: Primary action button must be reachable via keyboard and have an accessible name

reproductionSteps: Note HIDDEN flag in layout dump output
suggestedFix: Verify that btn-end-turn is not rendered with visibility:hidden or pointer-events:none during the player_action phase. Remove conditional hiding if present.

title: HP text (hp-value) renders at 15px — below 16px minimum for HP numbers
description: The player HP display span.hp-value shows '100/100' at 15px font size. HP is the most critical stat in combat — the UX spec requires HP numbers to be at least 16px. At 15px it will be uncomfortable to read during fast-paced combat, especially on Steam Deck (7" screen at 1280×800).

evidence.required: 16px minimum for HP/critical stat numbers

reproductionSteps: Layout dump: (297, 15) 64x15 = 15px height confirms approximate font size
suggestedFix: Increase hp-value font-size to calc(16px * var(--text-scale, 1)) minimum

title: Enemy intent bubble flagged HIDDEN during combat — intent info may be inaccessible
description: The div.enemy-intent-bubble is flagged HIDDEN in the layout dump at (396, 302) 234x100, yet it visually renders with 'Swooping strike — Attacking for 14 damage'. If the HIDDEN state is CSS-driven (not JS visibility), it may not be present in the accessibility tree. Enemy intent is core strategic information.

evidence.measured: Flagged HIDDEN in layout dump, text 'Swooping strike' not in accessibility tree
evidence.required: Enemy intent information must be accessible (aria-live region or visible element)

reproductionSteps: Check accessibility tree for enemy intent information
Note HIDDEN flag in layout dump despite visual rendering
suggestedFix: Add aria-live='polite' region announcing enemy intent changes. Ensure intent bubble is not set to visibility:hidden when active.

title: Card type label (v2-card-type) renders at 11px or smaller — at threshold
description: The frame-text.v2-card-type element inside hand cards has dimensions of approximately 22x11 pixels, indicating a font size of around 11px. This is the bare minimum and may be unreadable at smaller viewport sizes or for players with moderate visual impairment. Card type (Attack, Block, Utility) is strategically important.

evidence.required: Body text minimum 11px, card type label should be clearly readable

suggestedFix: Increase card type label to calc(12px * var(--text-scale, 1)) minimum

title: AP counter (ap-number) is flagged HIDDEN — action point display may be inaccessible
description: The span.ap-number showing '3' (current AP) is flagged HIDDEN in the layout dump at position (0,0), along with ap-label 'AP'. The AP display is essential game information — players need to know how many actions they have. If this is rendered off-screen or hidden via CSS, players are losing critical information.

evidence.measured: Both flagged HIDDEN at position (0,0) — off-screen or visibility:hidden
evidence.required: AP counter must be prominently visible during player_action phase

reproductionSteps: Load combat-basic scenario in player_action phase
Look for AP counter display on screen
Layout dump shows span.ap-number at (0,0) HIDDEN
suggestedFix: Verify AP counter visibility logic. The div.player-ap-right.ap-active at (312, 999) shows a separate ap-num element — confirm this is the active display and that position is accessible.

title: Dungeon map renders blank/dark — map nodes exist in DOM but fog overlay covers everything
description: The dungeon map screenshot shows a nearly-black screen with faint nebula effects. All map nodes are present in the DOM (map-node-r0-n0 through r7-n0 at coordinates 694-1139 x 267-1233), but the fog-overlay at (-340, -10) 2600x1350 appears to cover the entire visible area. Players would see an empty black screen with no indication of map content.

evidence.required: At minimum the current floor entry nodes and the boss node should be visible without fog

reproductionSteps: Observe screenshot — nearly blank dark screen
Layout dump shows map nodes but all buried under fog-overlay
suggestedFix: Verify fog logic for floor 1 maps. Entry nodes at row 0 should be visible (not fogged) on initial load. Check if fog state was not initialized for the scenario preset.

title: All 18 map node buttons lack accessible names — node type not communicated
description: Every map node button (map-node-r0-n0 through map-node-r7-n0) has a testid but no text content, no aria-label, and no title. Players using keyboard or screen readers cannot determine which nodes are combat, rest, shop, or boss. The node type icons are visual-only. 18 interactive buttons with zero accessible information.

evidence.required: Each map node button should have aria-label describing node type and position (e.g. 'Row 3 node 1: Rest Site')

reproductionSteps: No aria-label, no text content found
suggestedFix: Add aria-label to each map node: `aria-label='{nodeType} — Floor {floor}'` where nodeType is combat/rest/shop/elite/boss

title: Card description text (mini-card-desc) renders at approximately 11px — critical info too small
description: The mini-card-desc spans showing 'Deal 8', 'Gain 6', 'Deal 20' have a bounding box of approximately 47x113 to 135x135 pixels at the reward screen card size. Given the card dimensions (517x675), the description text area (113x47 for 'Deal 8') indicates very small font. This is the primary decision-making information for card selection.

evidence.required: Body text minimum 11px; card effect description should be clearly readable at reward selection

suggestedFix: Verify card description font size uses calc(11px * var(--text-scale, 1)) minimum

title: Card title and heading are positioned off-screen (y=-157 and y=-63) — not visible
description: The h1 'Choose a Card' is at Y=-157 (off-screen top) and the p subtitle 'Cavern Altar...' is at Y=-63 (off-screen top). These contextual elements that help the player understand the screen are not visible on the 1920x1080 viewport. The reward screen loses its heading/context.

evidence.measured: h1 at (93, -157) 1725x86 — 157px above viewport; p at (93, -63) 1725x41 — 63px above viewport
evidence.required: Screen title and context text must be visible within the viewport

reproductionSteps: Layout dump shows h1 at (93, -157) — above the 1080 viewport top edge
Screenshot confirms no heading visible at top of card reward screen
suggestedFix: Fix card reward layout — scroll or adjust top offset so heading is visible. Currently heading requires negative viewport space. Remove negative margins or transform that push h1 off-screen.

title: Reroll button is 1800x90px spanning full width — deceptively large, no destructive gap from Skip
description: The reward-reroll button spans 1800x90 pixels (full width). The 'Skip' button is at Y=979 and the reroll button is at Y=871 — gap of approximately 19px between reroll bottom edge and skip top edge. This is below the 20px destructive gap requirement since skipping has no undo. The reroll button's extreme width may also cause accidental activation.

evidence.required: Destructive action gap ≥ 20px between reroll and skip/accept buttons

reproductionSteps: Measure gap between reroll bottom (871+90=961) and skip top (979)
suggestedFix: Increase spacing between reroll and skip/accept button row to at least 20px

title: Reward room Continue button is a Phaser Text object — not accessible to DOM at all
description: The reward room 'Continue' button is a Phaser Container with a Text child at (960, 950) — it exists only in the Phaser canvas layer, not the DOM layer. There is no DOM button, no aria role, no keyboard interaction possible. This is the sole action on the reward room screen. Players who cannot use a mouse have no way to proceed.

evidence.measured: Phaser Container at (960, 950) 270x72 — no corresponding DOM element
evidence.required: All interactive buttons must exist in the DOM for accessibility and keyboard navigation

reproductionSteps: Search DOM for any button, [role=button], or interactive element
Layout dump DOM layer shows only topbar elements — no Continue DOM button
suggestedFix: Add a DOM-overlay button positioned over the Phaser canvas that calls the same reward-room completion handler. Pattern: transparent button overlaid on the Phaser button position.

title: Shop buy buttons are 74x66px — width barely meets minimum but gap to content is poor
description: The three shop buy buttons (shop-buy-relic-whetstone, iron_shield, vitality_ring) measure 74x66 pixels. Height 66px passes the 44px minimum but the gap between the relic item card (which ends at ~502px) and the buy button (starts at ~568px) gives only ~66px of internal spacing. The 66px height means accidental taps between item art and price button are likely.

evidence.required: 44x44px minimum; recommend 44x44 or larger with adequate gap from item content

suggestedFix: Consider expanding buy buttons to at least 80x48px for better ergonomics. The current 66px height passes minimum but benefits from additional height.

title: Shop relic items have emoji-only icon with no accessible name for the relic
description: Shop relic items display an emoji icon (🪨, 🛡️, 💍) and a rarity pill ('common'), but no accessible name for the relic itself. The relic name is not in the visible DOM elements listed in the layout dump — only the icon and rarity pill are present. Players using assistive technology cannot identify which relic they are looking at.

evidence.measured: Emoji icon only — no accessible name for relic in DOM scan
evidence.required: Each shop item should have a visible or aria-accessible name

reproductionSteps: No relic name text in layout dump — only icon emoji and rarity pill
suggestedFix: Add aria-label to each relic article (e.g. aria-label='Whetstone — Common relic'). Also verify relic name is visually present (may be hidden in screenshot crop).

title: Card Transform service shows 'Coming soon' but button is still interactive
description: The Card Transform service button renders with text 'Coming soon' for its price, but the button itself (service-action) still renders as an active button (not disabled). Players who click it expecting a feature will experience silent failure. The service should be visually grayed out with a disabled attribute.

evidence.measured: Active button with 'Coming soon' price label — no disabled state
evidence.required: Non-functional features should be disabled (aria-disabled=true, visual gray state)

reproductionSteps: Layout dump shows active button despite 'Coming soon' status
suggestedFix: Add disabled attribute and aria-disabled='true' to Card Transform button. Add tooltip explaining feature is in development.

title: Rest option detail text (option-detail) renders at approximately 13-14px — borderline
description: The option-detail spans ('Heal 20% HP', 'Quiz 3 questions...', 'Remove 1 card from your deck') have bounding boxes around 108x25 to 197x76. The 25px height for single-line text suggests approximately 13-14px font size, which is above the 11px minimum but may be uncomfortable at distance on a monitor.

evidence.required: Body text minimum 11px; important decision text benefits from 14px+ for comfortable reading

suggestedFix: Confirm font size is calc(13px * var(--text-scale, 1)). Consider bumping to 14px for better readability.

title: Study option shows 'No cards to upgrade' but no visual disabled state on the button
description: The rest-study button renders fully active even when 'No cards to upgrade' is shown as the preview text. Players will click Study expecting to upgrade cards but receive a quiz with no upgrade reward. The button should either be disabled or the warning should be more prominent (e.g. in red, with a warning icon).

evidence.measured: Button fully active at 243x312, preview text 'No cards to upgrade' at 156x23
evidence.required: When a choice has reduced/no effect, the player should see a clear affordance difference

reproductionSteps: Load rest-site scenario with no upgradeable cards
Observe Study option — button fully active
Preview reads 'No cards to upgrade' in same style as normal preview
suggestedFix: Add a warning color (amber/yellow) to the preview text when effect is null. Consider dimming the option card or adding a badge.

title: Run stats pill labels are 14px on small pill boxes — may be unreadable on Steam Deck
description: The run stats section shows pill-label spans ('Floor', 'Accuracy', 'Best Chain', 'Encounters', 'Time') measuring approximately 14x42 to 87x14 pixels. The 14px height indicates approximately 10-11px font size. On Steam Deck (7 inch at 1280x800 scaled) these labels will be extremely small.

evidence.required: Body text minimum 11px, label context text minimum 10px acceptable but push toward 11

suggestedFix: Increase pill-label to calc(11px * var(--text-scale, 1)) minimum

title: Multiple enemy badge images show '❓' fallback — missing enemy portraits on run end screen
description: At least 5 enemy badge-wrapper elements show span.enemy-badge-fallback '❓' as the visible element while the img.enemy-badge-img is HIDDEN. This means multiple enemies in the 'Foes Vanquished' parade show blank question marks instead of their portraits. This is a significant visual regression on the victory screen.

evidence.required: All enemy badges should show portraits from enemy sprite assets

reproductionSteps: Multiple enemies show '❓' symbol instead of portraits
Layout dump confirms: enemy-badge-img HIDDEN with badge-fallback visible for several enemies
suggestedFix: Verify enemy badge image paths in run-end-victory scenario. Ensure enemy sprite textures are loaded before the run end screen renders. Add a loading state to defer display until images load.

title: Deck selection panels (Trivia Dungeon, Study Temple) have no interactive button — entire panel is a div
description: The deck selection hub shows two large panels (panel--trivia, panel--study) each 750x720px, but neither has a button or role='button' element. The click handler likely lives on the div.panel itself. This means keyboard navigation cannot reach the panels, and screen readers will not announce them as interactive. There is only one DOM button on this screen: the back button.

evidence.measured: div elements at 750x720px — no button, no role attribute
evidence.required: Interactive areas must use button elements or role='button' with keyboard handlers

reproductionSteps: Press Tab to navigate — only back-btn is reachable via keyboard
Layout dump: no button elements for the two dungeon panels
suggestedFix: Wrap each panel div in a button element, or add role='button' and tabindex='0' with keydown Enter/Space handlers. Add aria-label='Trivia Dungeon — Battle with knowledge' etc.

title: Deck selection panel text overlay has no visible text in DOM scan — title only rendered via CSS/canvas
description: The panel-text-overlay divs within each panel don't contain visible text in the DOM scan despite the screenshot showing 'TRIVIA DUNGEON', 'The Armory', and description text. This suggests the text is rendered via CSS content, canvas, or Phaser layer — making it invisible to accessibility tools.

evidence.measured: div.panel-text-overlay at (374, 454) 385x269 — no child text elements in DOM scan
evidence.required: Visible text content should be in the DOM for accessibility

reproductionSteps: DOM scan shows empty divs despite visible text in screenshot
suggestedFix: Add actual text elements inside panel-text-overlay (h2 for dungeon name, p for description). These can be visually positioned over the art using absolute positioning.

title: Settings checkboxes (SFX Enabled, Music Enabled, Ambient Enabled) are not visible as DOM buttons
description: The settings screen shows three toggle checkboxes but none appear in the DOM as interactive elements in the layout dump. Only the tab buttons and labels are captured. If the checkboxes use native input[type=checkbox] without proper styling or a custom component without button semantics, they may have very small hit targets (~20x20px native checkbox).

evidence.measured: No interactive elements found for toggles in layout dump
evidence.required: Toggle controls minimum 44x44px tap target

reproductionSteps: Layout dump shows only span text elements — no button or input for toggles
suggestedFix: Verify checkbox components use custom button-style toggles with min 44x44px click area. Add explicit click area wrapper div with minimum size.

title: Settings page bottom half is empty — large dead space below Audio section
description: The settings Audio tab fills only the top ~530px of 1080px height. The bottom 550px is empty dark space. This makes the settings page feel unfinished and wastes screen real estate. On Steam Deck or ultrawide displays this empty space is even more pronounced.

evidence.measured: Content ends around Y=650 of 1080 — 430px empty space below
evidence.required: Settings content should use the available space or have a clear visual boundary

reproductionSteps: Observe the large empty space below the audio section
Screenshot shows approximately 40% of screen height unused
suggestedFix: Either: add a decorative separator/card boundary at the content end, or expand settings card to use max-content height with a scroll container.

title: FPS drops to 10-15 sustained after 5+ turns of combat under Docker SwiftShader
description: Sustained combat FPS drops from initial 31fps to 10-15fps after approximately 5 turns. This pattern was previously documented as HIGH-4 (2026-04-10) and should be interpreted with Docker-SwiftShader baseline in mind. The initial FPS is above the 22fps SwiftShader ceiling, which indicates the game loads within budget. The 10-15fps sustained reading likely reflects SwiftShader resource exhaustion under simultaneous multi-container load (16 containers at peak during this batch). drawCalls=-1 confirms SwiftShader GL draw call counting is disabled. On real GPU hardware this is NOT expected to reproduce. CRITICAL CONTEXT: System load average was 35-125 during this batch; this is NOT a representative isolated measurement.
suggestedFix: No fix needed for Docker baseline. For real GPU: verify SwiftShader detection in probeGPU() fires correctly (see HIGH-4 fix). Monitor on real hardware before actioning.

title: Docker SwiftShader WebGL drawCalls always -1 (not observable in this environment)
description: The phaserPerf.drawCalls field returns -1 in Docker SwiftShader. The GL draw call count (renderer.gl?.drawCount) is not available when SwiftShader is running. This means draw call budgeting cannot be validated in Docker. Real GPU testing required for draw call verification.
suggestedFix: Document in perf-baselines.md that drawCalls=-1 is expected in Docker SwiftShader. Validate draw call count on native GPU only.

title: Docker SwiftShader page crashes under extreme multi-container load (16+ parallel containers)
description: When 16 Docker warm containers are running simultaneously (BATCH-2026-04-11-ULTRA parallel batch), the SwiftShader GPU process experiences SharedImageManager mailbox errors and the Chromium renderer crashes. The error pattern is: repeated 'SharedImageManager::ProduceSkia: Trying to Produce a Skia representation from a non-existent mailbox' followed by 'Target crashed'. This is a Docker host resource exhaustion issue, not a game bug. However it documents that parallel Docker testing at scale >8 containers on this host is unreliable.

evidence.errorText: SharedImageManager::ProduceSkia: Trying to Produce a Skia representation from a non-existent mailbox (repeated 30+ times)

reproductionSteps: Attempt to run any test in any container
Page crashes with Target crashed error
suggestedFix: Limit parallel Docker containers to max 8. Stagger batch track start times by 2-3 minutes. Consider sequential Docker testing for stress/perf track specifically.

title: PASS: 50 rapid quickPlayCard+endTurn iterations produce zero errors or race conditions
description: Rapid input mashing (50 play+endTurn cycles with no delay) produced zero game errors, zero race conditions, and no UI errors. All 50 plays and 50 turn ends executed successfully. The game handles rapid automated input gracefully.

title: Viewport resize via JS dispatch does not change --layout-scale (expected: Playwright viewport is fixed)
description: window.dispatchEvent(new Event('resize')) with no actual viewport size change does not alter --layout-scale (stayed at 1.5). This is expected behavior - the CSS variable is driven by the actual viewport dimensions, not artificial resize events. In Playwright, window.resizeTo() is sandboxed and ignored. Actual resize testing requires Playwright viewport parameter change or multiple viewport configurations. The canvas getBoundingClientRect returned 42x28px which is mid-animation transform (not a bug - per testing.md Layout dump is not ground truth). Screenshot at 1920x1080 shows correct layout with all elements in frame.
suggestedFix: For future resize testing, use Playwright's page.setViewportSize() API or test at multiple viewport sizes via cold Docker runs with different --viewport parameters.

title: PASS: Shop renders correctly at 500g with 3 items; no zero-gold scenario available via __rrPlay
description: Shop scenario renders correctly: shows 500g gold display, 3 items (Whetstone 150g, Iron Shield 150g, Vitality Ring 150g). The __rrPlay API does not expose a setGold() method, so forcing 0-gold state requires a different approach (spawn with gold:0 parameter). Shop did not crash. forceHand() is also not in __rrPlay API - empty hand state cannot be force-tested via current API. These are API coverage gaps, not game bugs.
suggestedFix: Consider adding __rrPlay.setGold(amount) and __rrPlay.forceHand([]) to the debug API for more comprehensive edge state testing.

title: PASS: Game handles corrupt localStorage save gracefully - loads hub without crash
description: Corrupting 4 of 5 localStorage save keys (rr_save, rr_analytics_queue, terra-review-prompt-state, card:currentScreen) with invalid JSON did not crash the game. Hub scene loaded normally after corruption. Combat scenario also loaded normally. This confirms robust save data error handling - the game falls back to defaults when save data is corrupt.

title: PASS: Memory stable across 3 consecutive combats (350-353MB, <1% growth)
description: Memory usage across 3 consecutive combat scenario loads: combat1_start=350.3MB, combat1_end=351.0MB, combat2_start=350.2MB, combat2_end=352.6MB, combat3_start=353.2MB, combat3_end=352.6MB. Maximum growth: 2.9MB (0.83%) across 3 combats - well below the 10% threshold for leak suspect. gameObjectCount=36 and textureCount=109 were stable across all 3 combats, confirming proper cleanup on scenario reload.

title: PASS: Zero game logic errors in __rrLog and recentErrors across combat/shop/reward/hub
description: Across combat-basic, shop, reward-room, and hub-fresh scenarios, window.__rrLog.filter(e=>e.type==='error') returned 0 entries and window.__rrDebug().recentErrors returned [] in all cases. The 4 console errors collected were all ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED for static asset fetches (Docker environment artifact) and 1 Web Worker blob URL warning. Zero game logic errors.

evidence.consoleErrorBreakdown: 3x ERR_CONNECTION_REFUSED (Docker static asset), 1x Web Worker blob URL warning
